code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a fluent single sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # More natural template phrases – these produce only the predicate part,
    # the subject will be added once per entity.
    tmpl = {
        "league": "plays in the {o} league",
        "manager": "is managed by {o}",
        # Use a neutral phrasing for position to match expected references.
        "position": "position is {o}",
        "ground": "home ground is {o}",
        "birthDate": "was born on {o}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {o}",
        "birthYear": "was born in {o}",
        "club": "plays for {o}",
        "draftTeam": "was drafted by {o}",
        "height": "is {o} m tall",
        "youthclub": "played youth football for {o}",
        "currentclub": "currently plays for {o}",
        "weight": "weighs {o} kg",
        "deathPlace": "died in {o}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": "started his active years in {o}",
        "currentteam": "currently plays for {o}",
        "debutTeam": "made his debut with {o}",
        "draftPick": "was pick number {o}",
        "draftRound": "was selected in round {o}",
        "formerTeam": "formerly played for {o}",
        "playerNumber": "wears number {o}",
        "college": "attended {o}",
        "draftYear": "was drafted in {o}",
        "coach": "is coached by {o}",
        "owner": "is owned by {o}",
        "city": "is based in {o}",
        # Prefer a direct statement rather than a passive construction.
        "leader": "has leader {o}",
        "anthem": "has anthem {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group {o}",
        "language": "has language {o}",
        "generalManager": "has general manager {o}",
        "chairman": "has chairman {o}",
        "season": "participated in the {o} season",
        "currency": "uses the {o} currency",
        "demonym": "people are called {o}",
        "officialLanguage": "official language is {o}",
        "stadium": "stadium is {o}",
        "universityTeam": "university team is {o}",
        "mostChampions": "has most championships: {o}",
        "leaderTitle": "leader title is {o}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {o}",
        "country": "is in {o}",
        "utcOffset": "UTC offset is {o}",
        "timeZone": "time zone is {o}",
        "areaTotal": "total area is {o}",
        "foundingDate": "was founded on {o}",
    }

    # Group predicates by subject so we can mention the subject only once.
    subject_phrases: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.predicate not in tmpl:
            continue
        phrase = tmpl[t.predicate].format(o=t.object)
        subject_phrases.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(phrase)

    # Build sentences per subject, removing duplicate phrases.
    subject_sentences: list[str] = []
    for subj, phrases in subject_phrases.items():
        # deduplicate while preserving order
        seen = set()
        uniq_phrases = [p for p in phrases if not (p in seen or seen.add(p))]
        if not uniq_phrases:
            continue
        if len(uniq_phrases) == 1:
            clause = f"{subj} {uniq_phrases[0]}"
        else:
            # join with commas and an 'and' before the last phrase
            clause = f"{subj} " + ", ".join(uniq_phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + uniq_phrases[-1]
        subject_sentences.append(clause)

    # Combine all subject clauses into one final sentence.
    if not subject_sentences:
        return ""
    if len(subject_sentences) == 1:
        return subject_sentences[0] + "."
    else:
        return "; ".join(subject_sentences) + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
